title: Fiery Charred Peruvian Lamb Stew
description: A creamy and charred Peruvian lamb stew made with lamb shoulder and a blend of spices, perfect for a family dinner. This dish is kid-friendly and incorporates ayurvedic sattvic principles.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the lamb shoulder pieces with salt, black pepper, and peruvian spices.
Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Char the lamb shoulder pieces until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Remove the lamb from the pot and set aside.
Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Add the chopped aji amarillo pepper and cook for 2 minutes.
Add the heavy cream to the pot and stir to combine with the onion and pepper mixture.
Return the lamb to the pot and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Cover the pot and transfer to the preheated oven.
Cook for 2.5 hours, or until the lamb is tender and the sauce has thickened.
